_notes: Three-layer appearance: build (race-aware), feature (face/body detail), distinctive (the memorable thing). All phrases are lowercase — the generator capitalises the first word at render time.

build._notes: Physical frame and posture. Race-specific pools used when available; shared pool is fallback.
shared: average in height with nothing immediately remarkable about their frame
tall and lean, carrying themselves with quiet economy of movement
broad-shouldered and solid, built like someone who works for a living
short and compact, with a low centre of gravity
rangy and long-limbed, all angles and reach

aasimar: tall with an otherworldly stillness to how they hold themselves
graceful and luminous-skinned, as if lit from somewhere inside
lean with a posture that commands attention without seeming to try
slender and composed, radiating a calm that doesn't feel entirely earned
tall and unhurried — the kind of presence that quiets a room
dragonborn: powerfully built with a draconic frame that fills most doorways
broad-shouldered and imposing, scales catching the light as they move
tall and heavyset with thick neck scales and a deliberate gait
massive through the chest with forearms built for serious work
upright and commanding, with a natural authority in how they stand
dwarf: stocky and barrel-chested, every inch of them dense muscle
compact and solid, built close to the ground like bedrock
broad through the shoulders with a low centre of gravity and sure footing
short and powerfully built, with hands that could bend iron
squat and immovable-looking, as if they grew from the stone themselves
elf: slender and willowy with angular proportions and unhurried grace
tall and lean with a dancer's posture and economy of movement
lithe and sharp-edged, every gesture precise and considered
long-limbed and spare, carrying themselves as if the ground barely matters
slim and still, with a quietness to their presence that can unsettle
gnome: tiny and wiry with quick, darting movements and a restless energy
slight of frame but with surprising dexterity in their hands
small and compact, taking up almost no space and somehow always in the way
round-faced and small, with bright eyes that move faster than the rest of them
barely there physically, but their presence runs ahead of them
goliath: massive and towering, built on a different scale than most people
enormous — a full head taller than most humans and proportionally wider
powerfully muscled with stone-grey skin and the stillness of something geological
huge and deliberate, moving with a care that seems learned from breaking things
imposing enough that people move without realising they've done it
halfling: short and stout with a comfortable, round-faced warmth
small and nimble, taking up almost no space in a room
plump and unhurried in movement, with an easy, settled presence
compact and light on their feet in a way that sneaks up on people
small-framed and soft-featured, built for comfort and concealment equally
human: average in height with nothing immediately remarkable about their frame
tall and broad-shouldered, carrying themselves with earned confidence
lean and angular, with the kind of build that comes from not eating enough
short and stocky, solid without being heavy
medium-built and forgettable at a glance, which suits them fine
orc: muscular and broad, radiating physical presence without trying to
hulking and heavy-set with thick arms and a low, heavy brow
powerfully built and dense, the kind of frame that stops fights before they start
wide through the shoulders with a slightly forward lean, like they're always ready
massive in a way that seems architectural — as if they were constructed rather than born
tiefling: lean and angular with a coiled quality to their stillness
lithe with a tail that betrays their mood before their face does
slender and long-fingered, with a grace that makes people uncomfortable
slim and composed, with an economy of movement that reads as dangerous
spare and controlled, carrying themselves like they've learned not to take up space

feature: _notes: facial and body features — the second layer
sharp cheekbones that catch every shadow
deep-set eyes under a heavy brow
a wide, expressive jaw that leads their expressions
a high forehead and a composure that rarely breaks
a hooked nose and watchful eyes that don't miss much
a broad, flat nose and wide-set eyes
thin lips that rarely move to full expression
full lips and an easy, disarming mouth
a square jaw and a direct gaze that holds a beat too long
close-set eyes that give the impression of constant evaluation
heavy-lidded eyes that give away nothing
a long, angular face with prominent bones
a round face that makes them look younger than they are
a strong brow ridge that keeps their eyes in permanent shadow
weather-beaten features with deep lines that map a hard life
smooth, almost ageless skin that makes their age hard to guess
a hard mouth that softens rarely and only when they mean it
an open face that makes dishonesty difficult and obvious
dark eyes that reflect light in an unsettling way
a prominent scar bisecting one eyebrow
a jaw set slightly off-centre from an old break
eyes so pale they look almost colourless in direct light
heavy, expressive brows that communicate before they speak
a thin, sharp nose that gives their profile a blade-like quality
an unusually still face — emotions that arrive and depart without announcement

distinctive._notes: The one thing you remember. Shared pool used for all races; race-specific details added when relevant.
shared: a jagged scar running from jaw to ear on the right side
mismatched eyes — one dark brown, one pale grey
a missing finger on the left hand, the wound old and clean
dense geometric tattoos covering both forearms
a burn scar on the neck, clearly from something serious and not talked about
hair gone completely white despite their apparent youth
a crooked nose that has been broken at least twice
a permanent squint in the left eye from an old injury
eyes that pick up light in a way that reads as unnatural
an elaborate brand on the right wrist — deliberate, not accidental
ceremonial scarring across the forehead in a pattern that means something specific
a single streak of pure silver running through otherwise dark hair
calloused hands that don't match the rest of their appearance
a small circular scar at the base of the throat
hands noticeably too large for the rest of their frame
a habit of keeping their hands very still when they feel they're being observed
always dressed as if they're about to leave somewhere
an accent that doesn't quite match where they claim to be from
teeth filed to points in the back — visible only when they actually smile
a knuckle scar pattern that tells a story without words
one ear notched, the cut precise and old
eyes that change shade depending on the light — or the mood
a faint smell of smoke that has nothing to do with proximity to fire

dragonborn: scales that shift between two colors at the jawline — a bloodline mixing
a crest of small ridge-like horns along the crown that they keep polished
a tail with an unusual kink from an old break that never set right
scales with a metallic sheen that doesn't match their lineage's coloring
tiefling: small curved horns worn down to smooth nubs — filed deliberately or over time
a tail with an arrowhead tip that moves independently of their mood
solid-color eyes with no visible iris — the effect is unsettling up close
faint arcane markings along the jaw and collarbone that glow when they're angry
aasimar: a faint golden luminescence to the skin that is only visible in dim light
eyes that flash briefly with inner light when strong emotion surfaces
hair that drifts slightly in air that isn't moving
a warmth to the skin that has nothing to do with temperature
goliath: stone-pattern markings across the scalp and neck that form a tribal record
lithoderms that trace the map of every significant injury they've survived
a patch of almost translucent skin over the left shoulder where something burned through
elf: ears significantly longer than most elves — an old bloodline making itself known
eyes that shift color slightly depending on emotional state, uncontrollably
hair that moves as if in water when they're very still
dwarf: a braid in their beard that marks a specific oath — never explained unless asked directly
rune-work tattooed across the backs of the hands that they keep covered in company
a dent in one side of the skull, healed over but visible when the hair is short
orc: tusk tips capped in iron — a sign of something done or something owed
ritual scarring across the shoulders in a pattern specific to their clan
a brand over the heart that they don't cover and don't explain
